MTN TO DENY NTC OF HEARING ON DEBTOR'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION FILED BY DEFENDANT ZHANG, HAN

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT HAN ZHANG'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED IN PART. THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR CLAIMS THAT THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR DOES NOT ACTUALLY OWE THE MONEY THE SUBJECT OF THE JUDGMENT. THAT CLAIM CANNOT BE ADJUDICATED IN THIS PROCEEDING REGARDING CLAIMS OF EXEMPTION. THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR DOES NOT PROVIDE AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT TIMING ISSUES NOTED IN THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S "MOTION TO DENY" SUFFICES TO CANCEL THE PRESENT PROCEEDING. THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR CAN AFFORD $80 PER PAY PERIOD ($160 PER MONTH) AND THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED TO THAT EXTENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. of the Law Office of Elizabeth MacDonald, Tel. 415.321.9025, appearing specially for plaintiff.

No other appearance. DEFENDANT HAN ZHANG'S Claim Of Exemption is denied in part. The judgment debtor claims that the judgment debtor does not actually owe the money the subject of the judgment. That claim cannot be adjudicated in this proceeding regarding claims of exemption. The judgment debtor does not provide authority for the proposition that timing issues noted in the judgment debtor's "motion to deny" suffices to cancel the present proceeding. The judgment debtor can afford $80 per pay period ($160 per month) and the claim of exemption is denied to that extent. Order signed. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
